title: Locked Out Of EUV, Huawei Says Lithography Was Never The Point — Aninews ep 15
desc: Daily AI news satire. Huawei announced the Tau (τ) Scaling Law at IEEE ISCAS Shanghai on May 25, 2026 — measuring chip progress by signal travel time instead of transistor size, with 381 chips already produced and a 1.4nm-equivalent density target by 2031, without using a 1.4nm process.

title: My AI Video Maker Followed the Wrong Rules Perfectly
desc: I built an AI video maker, then watched it make a video that confused me. The scary part: it wasn't ignoring the rules. It was following the wrong ones perfectly.

title: SpaceX Files Plans For A $119 Billion Chip Factory To Feed Musk's Four Other Companies — Aninews ep 14
desc: Daily AI news satire. SpaceX disclosed in a federal filing that Terafab, its planned chip factory outside Austin, could cost up to $119B across all phases — and every customer of the fab (SpaceX rockets, Tesla cars, Tesla robots, xAI data centers) is also an owner.

title: Anthropic buys Stainless, the toolmaker behind every major AI company
desc: Daily AI news satire. Anthropic acquires Stainless, the small dev-tools vendor that quietly generated the SDKs and MCP connectors used by OpenAI, Google, Cloudflare, Replicate, Runway and Anthropic itself.

title: OpenAI launches a self-serve ChatGPT Ads Manager
desc: Daily AI news satire. OpenAI opens a self-serve Ads Manager inside ChatGPT, after years of arguing that paying for ChatGPT meant the answers weren't shaped by advertisers.

title: Google closes in on Nvidia as world's most valuable company
desc: Daily AI news satire. Two years after Google was written off as having lost the AI race, Alphabet is closing in on Nvidia as the world's most valuable company.

title: giving an AI agent a key to your wallet — spoolcast dev log 8
desc: devlog about letting AI agents spend real money in PipelineCPC, and the fences that keep it from going wrong.

title: Cisco stock pops 15% on 4,000 layoffs and an AI order surge
desc: Daily AI news satire. Cisco cuts ~4,000 jobs, books $5.3B in AI infrastructure orders, raises guidance to $9B — and lays off the engineers who built the networks.

title: Anthropic vs OpenAI: 271 bugs found, three of them real
desc: Daily AI news satire. Rival labs launch competing cybersecurity AIs — Claude Mythos flags 271 Firefox bugs (3 real), OpenAI answers with Daybreak.

title: three-ai AdsMetri build story — spoolcast dev log 7
desc: devlog about building AdsMetri across Codex, Manus, and Claude, and why role clarity mattered more than raw AI output.

title: Nvidia invests $40B in companies that spend on Nvidia
desc: Daily AI news satire. Nvidia pours $40B into companies whose business model is buying more Nvidia GPUs.

title: Google's AI Health Coach asks for your sleep data and $9.99
desc: Daily AI news satire. Google turns Fitbit into Google Health, launches a Gemini-powered coach behind a $9.99/mo gate. Not for medical purposes.

title: why ai makes things up about its own work — spoolcast dev log 6
Elon leases his data center to the AI company he once criticized
desc: Daily AI news satire. Anthropic gets all 220K GPUs of Colossus 1; xAI dissolves into SpaceXAI; the rivals are now roommates planning compute in orbit.

title: repo gps for ai agents — spoolcast dev log 5
Apple pays $250M for Siri features that didn't ship
desc: Daily AI news satire. Apple settles for $250M over a Siri 2.0 it announced at WWDC 2024 but never shipped. The new one ships at WWDC 2026 — powered by Google's Gemini.

title: Tesla targets 10M Optimus robots — currently folding laundry
desc: Daily AI news satire. Humanoid robot factories ramping; the early units fold one shirt at a time. Wall Street pegs the market at $13B by 2029.

title: google + amazon's anthropic markup — aninews ep 3
desc: Daily AI news satire. Half of Google's and Amazon's Q1 AI profit came from marking up their stake in Anthropic.

title: the bug an AI couldn't catch — spoolcast dev log 4
Microsoft to spend $120B on AI this year, used by 3% of customers
desc: Daily AI-generated tech news satire. Microsoft Q3 earnings + Copilot adoption.

title: OpenAI's $600B compute commitments and the $700B AI bill
desc: Pilot episode of the daily AI news satire show. OpenAI Q1 + hyperscaler capex.
